name: Moroccan Beef and Olive Tagine
description: A Moroccan beef and olive tagine built on French braising logic. Halal, deeply spiced with turmeric, ginger, and saffron, finished with preserved lemon and olives. Ready in 60 minutes.
keywords: halal french cooking, tagine, beef tagine, olive tagine, preserved lemon
recipeIngredient: 500 g lean halal beef, cut into 4-5 cm cubes
700 g waxy potatoes, peeled and cut into thick wedges
1 large handful green or red olives
1 small handful flat-leaf parsley or coriander

recipeInstructions.name: Make the spice base
recipeInstructions.text: In the base of your tagine or Dutch oven, combine the olive oil, sliced onion, garlic, ginger, turmeric, black pepper, salt, and dissolved saffron water. Stir to coat.

recipeInstructions.name: Add the beef
recipeInstructions.text: Place the beef cubes into the spice-and-onion mixture. Turn each piece so it picks up colour from the turmeric. Do not brown the beef first.

recipeInstructions.name: Layer the potatoes
recipeInstructions.text: Arrange the potato wedges on top of and around the beef. Pour the water over everything.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook, covered, on low heat
recipeInstructions.text: Place the lid on and cook over low-medium heat for 40 minutes. Do not lift the lid for the first 30 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Check doneness
recipeInstructions.text: At 40 minutes, a knife should meet the beef with no resistance. If not tender, cover and cook another 10 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Add olives and preserved lemon
recipeInstructions.text: Nestle the olives into the stew and lay the preserved lemon rind strips across the top. Cover and cook for a final 5 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Finish and serve
recipeInstructions.text: Taste for salt. Scatter chopped parsley or coriander over the top. Serve directly from the tagine or pot.
